title: Gas report - Week 17
summary:
LNG
The LNG market showed a notable shift this week, with a strong recovery in the Atlantic basin while the Pacific was mixed. On the BLNG1 Gladstone-Tokyo route, 174k cbm vessels declined by $1,000, closing at $22,200 per day, while 160k cbm vessels edged up by $200, settling at $13,800 per day. The continued decline for larger vessels highlights ongoing sluggishness in Pacific demand.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The LPG market has been relatively quiet this week, following the hectic, tariff-driven pricing of recent weeks. Fixing activity has slowed noticeably, and while rates remain below pre-tariff levels, the pace of decline has eased.
